hero.title: Durable task state for autonomous agent fleets
hero.sub: A task tracker for AI agents, shipped as one Zig binary with SQLite built in. Agents claim tasks by role over a plain JSON HTTP API, report progress, and move tasks through stages — so long-running work survives crashes and restarts without a human nudging it along.

alts.label: Build from source
alts.code:
git clone https://github.com/nullclaw/nulltickets
cd nulltickets
zig build
zig build run -- --port 7700 --db tracker.db

quickstart:
Start the tracker, then talk to it over plain JSON/HTTP.

```bash
# start the server (defaults: --port 7700, --db nulltickets.db)
zig build run -- --port 7700 --db tracker.db

# check service and queue health
curl http://127.0.0.1:7700/health

# fetch the OpenAPI 3.1 schema agents bootstrap from
curl http://127.0.0.1:7700/openapi.json

# run the test suite
zig build test
```

features.title: Lease-based claiming
features.body: An agent asks for the next task matching its role (POST /leases/claim) and gets it with a time-limited lease. Heartbeats extend the lease while the agent works; if the agent crashes, the lease expires and the task becomes claimable again. Lease tokens are stored as SHA-256 hashes, never plaintext.

features.title: Pipeline state machines
features.body: You declare which stages a task can be in and which moves between them are allowed — e.g. plan → review → done. The server rejects any transition that breaks those rules, and optional expected_stage / expected_task_version checks stop two agents from overwriting each other's updates.

features.title: Retries and dead letters
features.body: Per-task retry policy: max_attempts, retry_delay_ms, and an optional dead_letter_stage that catches exhausted tasks instead of losing them.

features.title: Dependencies and assignments
features.body: Mark task B as depending on task A, and B stays out of the claim queue until A reaches a finished stage. When it matters who does the work, you can also pin a task to one specific agent.

features.title: Shared agent memory
features.body: A namespaced key-value store with SQLite FTS5 full-text search and exact JSON filtering gives agents durable memory across sessions.

features.title: Self-describing API
features.body: GET /openapi.json returns the server's full OpenAPI 3.1 schema, so an agent can discover every endpoint at runtime instead of shipping a hand-written client. The server also accepts OpenTelemetry (OTLP) traces and links spans to tasks and runs.

note: Pre-1.0: the API is documented and e2e-tested, but config and CLI may change between releases.

plain.what: A single-binary HTTP service (written in Zig, SQLite embedded) that stores tasks for AI agents and hands them out through a JSON REST API — agents claim work by role, report progress events, and move tasks through defined stages.
plain.why: Long-running agent work normally dies with the process that runs it; nullTickets keeps the backlog, retries, and full history in SQLite on disk, so a crashed or restarted agent loop picks up where it left off instead of needing a human to restart it.
plain.when: You have a backlog too big for one session — say 100 tasks — and want an agent loop (nullclaw, the companion agent runtime, or any runtime that implements the claim → events → transition contract) to grind through it unattended, with failed tasks retried automatically.
